meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Learn how portfolio building helps you overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to master software development.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of pattern recognition. By shifting from 'fixing errors' to 'portfolio building,' you treat every bug as a documented feature. Use Antigravity to architect your logic and Claude Code to automate the trace, turning every roadblock into a permanent, career-advancing project asset.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal flickering with red error logs, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat you can’t revive. The frustration is palpable—that sinking feeling that you’ve hit a wall that no amount of Stack Overflow searching can break. You aren't just stuck; you're feeling the friction of moving from a tutorial-follower to a real-world builder.

But here is the secret: those bugs aren't obstacles; they are the raw material for your professional portfolio. When you stop viewing errors as failures and start viewing them as architectural challenges, you stop being a student and start being an engineer. We’re going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to flip the script, turning your debugging process into a high-value demonstration of technical competence.

steps.title: Architecting the Project Scope
steps.description: Define the boundaries of your app to ensure you aren't debugging the entire universe at once. Focus on one core feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Analyze my project requirements and break them down into 5 modular, testable components to minimize debugging complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --plan 'Break down project into modular components'

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to ingest your stack trace and identify the root cause patterns rather than just symptoms.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this stack trace and provide a root cause analysis, explaining the underlying architectural flaw that caused the crash.

steps.title: Documenting the Fix as a Feature
steps.description: Transform your debugging session into a technical blog post or README documentation segment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical breakdown of how I solved this bug, focusing on the trade-offs made and the architectural decision-making process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --chat 'Write a technical README section explaining this bug fix'

steps.title: Unit Test Implementation
steps.description: Prevent regression by writing automated tests for the code block you just fixed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for this module to ensure this bug never reappears in production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --chat 'Generate unit tests for this function'

steps.title: Refactoring for Readability
steps.description: Clean up your code to ensure it's portfolio-ready and readable for future employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this code for clean-code principles, readability, and performance optimizations. Suggest improvements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --chat 'Refactor this code for production-grade quality'

pro_tips: Always commit your buggy code separately before fixing it; it shows the 'Before' and 'After' evolution in your GitHub history.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Why' comments in your code, which demonstrates deeper thinking than just writing 'How' comments.
Treat every bug report as a ticket in a project management tool; it mimics real-world enterprise engineering workflows.
Focus on the 'Trade-offs'—when you explain why you chose one solution over another, you move from junior to senior territory.
Build a 'Debugging Log' repository where you curate the most complex bugs you've solved; it’s the best interview prep possible.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged and documented your current project, deploy it to a live environment using Vercel or Railway and add the link to your public portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are an engineer building a legacy. Every bug you squash is a credential earned. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
